label: PI-004 cohort closer ‚Äî `test_spec` methodology entity type end-to-end (migration, access, REST, UI, tests). Fourth and final of the four PI-004 sibling builds (others: field shipped under SES-083, requirement shipped under SES-084, manual_config shipped under SES-085). **This session RESOLVES PI-004** via slice A's atomic edge+flip behavior ‚Äî the `resolves_planning_items` section below carries one entry that flips PI-004 from Open ‚Üí Resolved at apply time. Migration 0017 creates the `test_specs` table with the three-status methodology lifecycle (candidate/confirmed/deferred, mirroring entity/domain per DEC-047) AND the four-value execution-outcome enum (not_run/passing/failing/skipped) on the same row ‚Äî the dual-axis state pattern per test_spec.md ¬ß3.4.3, the first v2 entity to ship with two independent enum axes. Methodology status uses the propose-verify gate (one-way out of `candidate`); execution outcome uses UNRESTRICTED transitions (observational, not decisional, per ¬ß3.4.2). Server enforces the ¬ß3.4.4 cross-field invariant ‚Äî `last_run_at` auto-populated when outcome moves to a run state, auto-cleared (along with `last_run_notes`) on move back to `not_run`, explicit-null `last_run_at` in a run state rejected with 422. Three new outbound reference kinds (test_spec_touches_entity, test_spec_touches_field, test_spec_exercises_process) registered in vocab.py and admitted in the refs CHECK; the previously-dormant `(requirement, test_spec)` clause in `_kinds_for_pair` is activated by this build (the `requirement_verified_by_test_spec` kind itself was already in REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS via SES-084's requirement build). Nine REST endpoints ‚Äî the eight standard plus POST /test-specs/{id}/record-run convenience per spec ¬ß3.8.1 (resolved affirmatively for v0.5+). UI ships the five-column master pane (Identifier/Name/Status/Last Run/Updated) with the color-cued Last Run column (passing green, failing red, not_run gray, skipped amber ‚Äî UI deviation per ¬ß3.6.2 justified by verification-health-at-a-glance), the three-section detail pane (identity-and-methodology / Test body / Last run + collapsible Internal notes + ReferencesSection), and the Record Run convenience-modal sub-dialog (transient ‚Äî uses deleteLater() per project_qt_worker_widget_gc_hazard.md). 49 new tests across access (26) + API (13) + UI (10). In-passing: test_smoke.py EXPECTED_ENTRIES + test_exporter.py expected files set extended; refresh.py filename mapping updated. Test suite: 1805 baseline ‚Üí 1854 (+49 new, 0 regressions), 3 skipped pre-existing. Resolves PI-004 per DEC-232 / SES-074 build-closure pattern: this is the closer; the cohort's `resolves` edge fires from this session to PI-004 atomically.

session.topics_covered: Execution of the build prompt's 17 numbered steps for the test_spec methodology entity per test_spec.md v1.0: (1) Alembic migration 0017 creating the `test_specs` table (fifteen columns per ¬ß3.2: identifier + eleven substantive ‚Äî name, description, setup, steps, expected, notes, status, last_run_outcome, last_run_at, last_run_notes ‚Äî plus three timestamps, three CHECK constraints ‚Äî identifier format / status enum / last_run_outcome enum, three indexes on status / last_run_outcome / deleted_at) and extending `refs.source_type` / `refs.target_type` / `refs.relationship_kind` (admitting all three new outbound test_spec_* kinds proactively per ¬ß3.3.1) / `change_log.entity_type` CHECKs in a single recopy; (2) `TestSpec` SQLAlchemy model in `access/models.py` after the `ManualConfig` class with parent-prefix field naming per DEC-046 and CheckConstraints for identifier format + status enum + last_run_outcome enum, three indexes; (3) vocab.py additions ‚Äî `TEST_SPEC_STATUSES` (3-value: candidate/confirmed/deferred per ¬ß3.4.1 ‚Äî same shape as entity/domain), `TEST_SPEC_STATUS_TRANSITIONS` (propose-verify gate), `TEST_SPEC_RUN_OUTCOMES` (4-value: not_run/passing/failing/skipped per ¬ß3.4.2 ‚Äî UNRESTRICTED transitions, **no transition map** with an explicit comment explaining why per ¬ß3.4.3 dual-axis rationale), `'test_spec'` added to `ENTITY_TYPES`, three test_spec_* kinds added to `REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS`, three new clauses added to `_kinds_for_pair`, AND the previously-dormant `(requirement, test_spec)` clause activated by uncommenting the TODO block (the `requirement_verified_by_test_spec` kind was already registered by the requirement build per CLAUDE.md line 48's once-per-kind rule), docstring updated; (4) `access/repositories/test_spec.py` repository with the eight standard functions plus the record_run convenience helper ‚Äî `_require_outcome` validates the four-value enum, `_apply_outcome_invariant` enforces the ¬ß3.4.4 cross-field invariant (clear-on-not_run, auto-set-on-run-state, reject-explicit-null-in-run-state), `_reject_duplicate_name` enforces global case-insensitive uniqueness per ¬ß3.2.1, SAVEPOINT-retry identifier auto-assignment per PI-002 / spec criterion 9, `_check_transition` mirrors the three-status propose-verify map (no equivalent for outcomes per ¬ß3.4.2); (5) `TestSpecCreateIn` / `TestSpecReplaceIn` / `TestSpecPatchIn` / `TestSpecRecordRunIn` Pydantic schemas ‚Äî the record-run schema's three fields (outcome required; notes / at optional) match the spec's ¬ß3.8.1 'thinner shape than PATCH' framing; (6) `api/routers/test_specs.py` with nine endpoints under `/test-specs` (hyphenated URL plural per ¬ß3.5.1); PATCH router strips `test_spec_` prefix and forwards including outcome / last_run_at fields; POST and PUT pass `last_run_at_supplied=('test_spec_last_run_at' in provided)` for the explicit-null-vs-omitted signal load-bearing for the ¬ß3.4.4 invariant; (7) main.py router registration adjacent to manual_configs under the `# PI-004 methodology cohort` block; (8) nine client methods in ui/client.py (eight standard + record_test_spec_run); (9) sidebar entry inserted into the Methodology group between Requirements and CRM Candidates per spec ¬ß3.6.1 proposed position; (10) main_window dispatch + ENTITY_TYPE_TO_SIDEBAR_LABEL entry + `test_specs.json` filename mapping in refresh.py; (11) `TestSpecsPanel` mirroring `ManualConfigPanel` with test_spec-specific adjustments ‚Äî five-column master pane per AC-12 (Identifier/Name/Status/Last Run/Updated), the **color-cued Last Run column** per ¬ß3.6.2 deviation (small `_LastRunColorDelegate` subclass installed via `setItemDelegateForColumn` on column 3 only ‚Äî leaves the shared `MasterPaneDelegate` driving the rest of the row; failing rows render in DemiBold weight), three-section detail pane per ¬ß3.6.3 with subsection headers 'Test body' and 'Last run' and a small color-matching swatch beside the outcome combo, Record Run action-strip button opening a transient TestSpecRecordRunDialog (uses `deleteLater()` per project_qt_worker_widget_gc_hazard.md), ReferencesSection rendering all three outbound kinds plus inbound `requirement_verified_by_test_spec`; (12) `TestSpecCreateDialog` / `TestSpecEditDialog` / `TestSpecDeleteDialog` / `TestSpecRecordRunDialog` + `_test_spec_schema.py` ‚Äî CreateDialog excludes identifier (server-assigned), EditDialog includes identifier as read-only, DeleteDialog uses edge-text confirmation per ¬ß3.6.6, status schema's `compute_options` restricts to valid successors (propose-verify gate), outcome schema's `compute_options` returns the full unconstrained four-value vocab (transitions unrestricted per ¬ß3.4.2). The dedicated RecordRunDialog (modal QDialog subclass) captures outcome + optional notes + optional at, POSTs to /test-specs/{id}/record-run on accept; surfaces server errors inline. FieldSchema does not currently support `widget=datetime` ‚Äî the build prompt's ¬ß12a fallback shipped: `widget=line` with an ISO-8601 placeholder for `test_spec_last_run_at`; the server enforces the ¬ß3.4.4 invariant regardless of what the dialog allows the user to type; (13) exporter.py gained `TestSpec` import and `('test_specs', TestSpec)` entry in `_EXPORT_TABLES`; (14) 49 new tests across three files ‚Äî 26 in tests/crmbuilder_v2/access/test_test_spec.py (criteria 1‚Äì10 + vocab registration smoke for all three outbound kinds + the activated requirement‚Üítest_spec inbound clause + record_run helper happy/error paths + ¬ß3.4.4 cross-field invariant in all three branches ‚Äî auto-set, auto-clear, explicit-null-rejection), 13 in tests/crmbuilder_v2/api/test_test_specs_api.py (criteria 7, 8 + envelope-on-422 for invalid status transition / cross-field invariant + record-run endpoint round-trip + soft-delete/restore + reference round-trip to entity), 10 in tests/crmbuilder_v2/ui/test_test_specs_panel.py (criteria 11, 12, 13 ‚Äî sidebar membership, five-column master, color-delegate attachment + per-outcome color distinctness, three subsection headers, Record Run button surface, dialog field-schema smoke); (15) live API smoke verification ‚Äî six end-to-end checks against the local server: POST creates TST-001 with status=candidate, outcome=not_run, last_run_at=null; PATCH outcome=passing without last_run_at auto-populates last_run_at to within seconds of now; PATCH outcome=not_run clears both last_run_at and last_run_notes; POST /test-specs/TST-001/record-run with outcome=failing+notes returns 200 with both fields updated; PATCH confirmed‚Üícandidate returns HTTP 422 with body `{error: 'invalid_status_transition', from: 'confirmed', to: 'candidate'}`; GET /test-specs/next-identifier returns the next TST-NNN. Smoke artifact TST-001 soft-deleted at session end per SES-083/SES-084/SES-085 precedent; (16) test suite: baseline 1805 passed ‚Üí 1854 passed / +49 new (3 skipped pre-existing, 0 regressions; constant-list assertions updated in passing: test_smoke.py EXPECTED_ENTRIES gained 'Test Specs', test_exporter.py expected-files set gained `test_specs.json`); (17) close-out authoring with the SES-086 / CONV-056 / DEC-284..287 identifier block.
session.summary: PI-004 cohort closer (`test_spec` methodology entity type) shipped end-to-end. **RESOLVES PI-004** via the resolves_planning_items section's atomic edge+flip behavior. The v2 storage layer now hosts test_spec records ‚Äî verification specifications that bridge requirements (what must be true) and verification runs (was it true on this deploy). The build mirrors the v0.4 entity / v0.5+ requirement / manual_config pattern with three bounded test_spec-specific characteristics per `test_spec.md` v1.0: (1) **dual-axis state** per ¬ß3.4.3 ‚Äî DEC-286 ‚Äî two enum fields on the row: `test_spec_status` (methodology lifecycle, restricted transitions per the propose-verify gate; same shape as domain/entity) and `test_spec_last_run_outcome` (execution outcome, UNRESTRICTED transitions per ¬ß3.4.2 because outcomes are observational, not decisional). The asymmetry IS the point ‚Äî the spec calls out methodology-lifecycle vs execution-outcome separation as a v0.5+ schema convention candidate for any future verification-like entity. (2) **¬ß3.4.4 cross-field invariant** ‚Äî DEC-286 ‚Äî `last_run_at` is auto-populated by the server when outcome moves to a run state (passing/failing/skipped), auto-cleared along with `last_run_notes` when outcome resets to `not_run`, and an explicit-null `last_run_at` while outcome is a run state returns 422. Enforced exclusively at the access layer via `_apply_outcome_invariant`; the API layer passes the explicit-null-vs-omitted signal via Pydantic's `model_dump(exclude_unset=True)`. (3) **`POST /test-specs/{id}/record-run` convenience endpoint** ‚Äî DEC-287 ‚Äî resolves the ¬ß3.8.1 open question in the affirmative; the thinner shape (just `{outcome, notes?, at?}`) is easier to call from automation than a three-field PATCH and aligns with the methodology-vs-execution principle. The PATCH endpoint still supports the same three-field update. Three outbound reference kinds per ¬ß3.3.1 ‚Äî `test_spec_touches_entity` (target=entity), `test_spec_touches_field` (target=field ‚Äî live as of PI-004 first slice), `test_spec_exercises_process` (target=process) ‚Äî DEC-287; all three targets are live, all three `_kinds_for_pair` clauses ship active. The inbound `requirement_verified_by_test_spec` kind was registered by the requirement build per the once-per-kind rule (CLAUDE.md line 48); this build only activates the previously-dormant `(requirement, test_spec)` clause by uncommenting it. Five-column master pane (Identifier/Name/Status/Last Run/Updated) per AC-12 ‚Äî DEC-287; the Last Run column ships with a color cue (passing green, failing red, not_run gray, skipped amber) per ¬ß3.6.2 UI deviation justified by verification-health-at-a-glance. Three-section detail pane (identity-and-methodology / Test body / Last run + collapsible Internal notes + ReferencesSection) per ¬ß3.6.3. Record Run convenience modal sub-dialog per ¬ß3.8.1 ‚Äî transient, uses `deleteLater()` per project_qt_worker_widget_gc_hazard.md. PI-004 resolves on this close-out per DEC-232 / SES-074 build-closure pattern.

conversation.conversation_description: Claude Code build-agent conversation at Doug's terminal, dispatched by a parent orchestrator session to execute the test_spec build prompt authored under SES-081 (WT-053). The agent followed the build prompt's 17 numbered steps in order, mirroring the manual_config-build pattern with test_spec-specific extensions per test_spec.md v1.0. Pre-flight identifier capture detected the heads (SES-085, DEC-283, CONV-055, WT-054); no parallel-sandbox collision. PI-004 addresses-edge count at session-open was 8 (4 conversations + 4 work_tickets), with three of the four conversations representing the prior sibling builds (CONV-053 field, CONV-054 requirement, CONV-055 manual_config) plus the design conversation (CONV-051) ‚Äî confirming this session is the PI-004 closer. The build's scope was purely additive (new table, new vocab, new endpoints) with one non-additive edit: the previously-dormant `(requirement, test_spec)` clause in `_kinds_for_pair` was activated by uncommenting the TODO block ‚Äî the kind itself was already in REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS via SES-084's requirement build per CLAUDE.md line 48's once-per-kind rule. The dual-axis state pattern (methodology-lifecycle restricted, execution-outcome unrestricted) is the first v2 entity to ship with two independent enum axes; the spec frames it explicitly as a v0.5+ convention candidate for any future verification-like entity. The ¬ß3.4.4 cross-field invariant on `last_run_at` is enforced at the access layer via `_apply_outcome_invariant`; the API layer routes the explicit-null-vs-omitted signal via Pydantic's `model_dump(exclude_unset=True)`. decisions.title: `test_spec` identifier prefix and format
decisions.context: PI-004 cohort closer build of the `test_spec` methodology entity type per test_spec.md v1.0 (¬ß3.1). The methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide ¬ß3.1 establishes a soft-3-letter prefix posture; the v2 prefix set already includes DEC, SES, RSK, PI, TOP, REF, CHR, STA, DOM, ENT, PROC, CRM, WS, CONV, RB, WT, COP, DEP, CM, PER, FLD, REQ, MCF. The entity name `test_spec` is two words.
decisions.decision: Adopt `TST` as the test_spec identifier prefix with format `TST-NNN` (3-digit zero-padded). Server-side identifier auto-assignment on POST omission per PI-002; `GET /test-specs/next-identifier` helper available per DEC-043. Explicit-identifier POST validated against `^TST-\d{3}$` with collision returning 409 and malformation returning 422.
decisions.rationale: Three letters of 'test' ‚Äî reads unambiguously as 'test', no collision against the existing prefix space (closest neighbour is STA = status; TST is letter-distinct at all three positions), matches the v2 governance- and methodology-entity norm. Avoids the two-letter TS form (reads as a generic timestamp abbreviation or topic_status) and the four-letter TEST form (one letter longer than the convention requires).
decisions.alternatives_considered: TS (two letters; rejected ‚Äî reads as a topic_status or generic timestamp abbreviation, less distinct from STA). TEST (four letters when three suffices; rejected ‚Äî breaks the soft-3-letter posture per DEC-044 without good cause). SPEC (rejected ‚Äî 'spec' is a v2-internal noun used in many contexts beyond test specs; ambiguous).
decisions.consequences: Test spec records are addressed as TST-001, TST-002, etc. Standard endpoint set with `GET /test-specs/next-identifier` helper per DEC-043. PI-002 server-assignment applies.

decisions.title: `test_spec` field inventory, dual-axis state, three plain-text body fields, v0.5+ minimum-viable scope
decisions.context: PI-004 cohort closer build per test_spec.md ¬ß3.2 / ¬ß3.9.1. Define the substantive fields and validation rules for v0.5+ shipping scope.
decisions.decision: Test spec carries eleven substantive fields plus inherited timestamps (fifteen total when timestamps are counted): `test_spec_identifier` (server-assigned), `test_spec_name` (required, case-insensitive global uniqueness), `test_spec_description` (required, plain text), `test_spec_setup` (optional, plain text ‚Äî preconditions), `test_spec_steps` (required, plain text ‚Äî steps to execute), `test_spec_expected` (required, plain text ‚Äî expected results), `test_spec_notes` (optional, consultant scratchpad), `test_spec_status` (required enum, default `candidate`), `test_spec_last_run_outcome` (required enum, default `not_run`), `test_spec_last_run_at` (nullable datetime ‚Äî server-enforced cross-field invariant per DEC-286), `test_spec_last_run_notes` (optional). Split the verification-spec body into THREE plain-text fields (setup / steps / expected) rather than a single `test_spec_body` per spec ¬ß3.2.2 ‚Äî setup, action, expected are the three things any verification run needs separately, and free-text-blob authoring tends to elide one of the three. The cost is three fields rather than one; the value is uniform shape that pays off in detail-pane rendering (¬ß3.6.3) and acceptance-criterion clarity. Plain text in v0.5+; markdown rendering deferred per ¬ß3.8.2. No storage-level length caps. Parent-prefix field naming per DEC-046.
decisions.rationale: Thinnest shape that faithfully hosts a verification specification (name + what + setup + steps + expected + consultant notes + lifecycle + last-run snapshot). Three-field body split matches the current-methodology Phase 13 verification-spec convention. Plain text is the cheap default; markdown is a v0.6+ candidate awaiting CBM-redo signal per ¬ß3.8.2.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Single `test_spec_body` free-text field (rejected; loses the setup/action/expected structural hint that drives detail-pane rendering and acceptance-criterion clarity). Structured numbered-substep JSON shape for `test_spec_steps` (rejected for v0.5; plain text is cheaper, structured shape deferred per ¬ß3.8.2 awaiting real-use signal). Length caps on text fields (rejected for v0.5+; pathological-input handling deferred to CBM-redo signal). Per-domain name uniqueness (rejected; a test spec is a globally-named verification, not domain-scoped).
decisions.consequences: Consultants author test specs with explicit setup / steps / expected fields. The detail pane renders them as three subsection-grouped widgets per ¬ß3.6.3. Cross-engagement reporting / future server-side filters (e.g., `?last_run_outcome=failing`) operate on flat scalar columns.

decisions.title: `test_spec` dual-axis state ‚Äî methodology lifecycle (restricted) and execution outcome (unrestricted) on the same row, with ¬ß3.4.4 cross-field invariant on `last_run_at`
decisions.context: PI-004 cohort closer build per test_spec.md ¬ß3.4 / ¬ß3.4.3 / ¬ß3.4.4 / ¬ß3.9.1. The domain (DEC-047) / entity (DEC-052) / persona (DEC-265) / field (DEC-270) / requirement (DEC-276) / manual_config (DEC-281) precedents established the three-status propose-verify lifecycle (manual_config added a fourth `completed` terminal). test_spec needs both a methodology lifecycle AND an execution-result axis; the naive shape would collapse them into one enum, which is wrong per ¬ß3.4.3's five-point justification.
decisions.decision: Test spec carries **two independent enum fields** on the same row, NOT one collapsed enum: (1) `test_spec_status` ‚àà `{candidate, confirmed, deferred}` ‚Äî methodology lifecycle, **restricted transitions** per the propose-verify gate (same shape as domain / entity per DEC-047): candidate ‚Üí confirmed or deferred (one-way gate); confirmed ‚Üî deferred; never regress to candidate. (2) `test_spec_last_run_outcome` ‚àà `{not_run, passing, failing, skipped}` ‚Äî execution outcome, **UNRESTRICTED transitions** per ¬ß3.4.2: any value freely follows any other, no transition map at all (the access-layer vocab module ships TEST_SPEC_RUN_OUTCOMES without a corresponding TEST_SPEC_RUN_OUTCOME_TRANSITIONS dict, with an explicit comment explaining why per the dual-axis rationale). The asymmetry IS the point: methodology lifecycle is decisional (does this test belong in the engagement?); execution outcome is observational (what happened on the last run?). The two move on independent cadences. **¬ß3.4.4 cross-field invariant** binds them: whenever `test_spec_last_run_outcome` is `passing` / `failing` / `skipped`, `test_spec_last_run_at` must be populated. Server enforces this at the access layer via `_apply_outcome_invariant`: outcome ‚Üí not_run clears `last_run_at` AND `last_run_notes` regardless of supplied values; outcome ‚Üí run state with `last_run_at` supplied non-null honors the value; outcome ‚Üí run state with `last_run_at` omitted server-defaults to `datetime.now(UTC)`; outcome ‚Üí run state with explicit-null `last_run_at` raises 422 (the invariant rejects). The API layer signals 'explicit-null-vs-omitted' via Pydantic's `model_dump(exclude_unset=True)` ‚Äî load-bearing for the ¬ß3.4.4 invariant. `test_spec_status` is independent of any inbound `requirement_verified_by_test_spec` references' source-requirement statuses. Soft-deleting a test_spec does NOT cascade-delete its outbound references (per ¬ß3.4.6).
decisions.rationale: Five-point justification per ¬ß3.4.3: (1) different lifecycles ‚Äî methodology vs execution move on independent cadences; (2) different transition semantics ‚Äî propose-verify gate vs frictionless update; (3) different rendering needs ‚Äî methodology lifecycle as plain enum, execution outcome with color cue; (4) different ownership ‚Äî consultant sets methodology, operator/automation sets outcome; (5) pattern reuse ‚Äî establishes a v0.5+ convention candidate for any future verification-like entity (the methodology-vs-execution principle that runs throughout v2's storage layer, e.g., apply-event / deposit-event in governance). Cost: one extra column plus its two run-fields companions (last_run_at, last_run_notes). Value: clean separation of concerns and a UI that can surface verification health without collapsing methodology and execution information. ¬ß3.4.4 cross-field invariant prevents the half-set state where outcome says 'passing' but timestamp is null ‚Äî a verification gap. Server-defaulting `last_run_at` to now() when outcome moves to a run state is the right ergonomics (operators don't have to author timestamps by hand) without losing the explicit-value escape hatch.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Collapse both axes into one enum `{candidate, confirmed, deferred, passing, failing, skipped}` (rejected; forces one set of transition rules to govern both axes ‚Äî either too restrictive for outcomes or too permissive for methodology status). Restrict outcome transitions to forward-only (rejected; a failing test going to passing after a fix is the most common transition, not regression ‚Äî the unrestricted shape matches the observational nature of outcomes). Make `last_run_at` storage-level NOT NULL (rejected; the cross-field rule is conditional on the outcome value, brittle in SQLite, and the access-layer body carries richer error detail). Inline outcome with status as one field (rejected per the dual-axis justification). Track outcome in a separate `test_run` entity from day one (rejected; v0.5+ snapshot shape is sufficient ‚Äî full history is a v0.6+ planning item per ¬ß3.8.3).
decisions.consequences: Status-transition validation in the access layer mirrors the three-status map; outcome validation is enum-membership-only (no transition check). The `_apply_outcome_invariant` helper is the load-bearing piece for the cross-field rule and runs from create / put / patch / record_run paths. Detail-pane UI renders the two combo boxes independently in two different subsection groups (identity-and-methodology block has status; Last run block has outcome). CRUD dialog's status combo restricts to valid successors via `compute_options`; outcome combo offers the full four-value vocab unconditionally. The ¬ß3.4.4 invariant fires on the explicit-null-vs-omitted signal carried by Pydantic's `exclude_unset=True` ‚Äî both POST/PUT/PATCH router and create_test_spec/update_test_spec/patch_test_spec repository functions pass the `last_run_at_supplied` boolean through. Future v0.6+ planning items: separate `test_run` history entity per ¬ß3.8.3; automated verification engine per ¬ß3.8.3; role-based outcome write affordance per ¬ß3.8.3.

decisions.title: `test_spec` API surface (ships convenience record-run endpoint), three outbound references, five-column master with color-cued Last Run, three-section detail pane, sidebar mid-Methodology position
decisions.context: PI-004 cohort closer build per test_spec.md ¬ß3.3.1 / ¬ß3.5 / ¬ß3.6 / ¬ß3.7 / ¬ß3.8.1 / ¬ß3.9.1.
decisions.decision: **Ship `POST /test-specs/{id}/record-run` at first release** ‚Äî resolves the ¬ß3.8.1 open question in the affirmative. Body shape `{outcome: str, notes: str | None = None, at: datetime | None = None}`; atomic outcome+at+notes update; access-layer helper `record_run` handles the ¬ß3.4.4 invariant (server-defaults `at` to now() when omitted; clears `last_run_at` and `last_run_notes` if outcome is `not_run` regardless of supplied values). The PATCH endpoint still supports the same three-field update; record-run is a thinner shape for automation callers and aligns with the methodology-vs-execution principle. Eight standard endpoints under `/test-specs` (hyphenated URL plural per ¬ß3.5.1) + the convenience record-run = nine total. **Three outbound reference kinds via the references entity**: `test_spec_touches_entity` (target=entity v0.4), `test_spec_touches_field` (target=field PI-004 first slice), `test_spec_exercises_process` (target=process v0.4). All three target entity types live; all three `_kinds_for_pair` clauses ship active unconditionally. The inbound `requirement_verified_by_test_spec` kind is **NOT registered by this build** per CLAUDE.md line 48's once-per-kind rule ‚Äî it was registered by the requirement build (DEC-277). This build activates the previously-dormant `(requirement, test_spec)` clause in `_kinds_for_pair` by uncommenting the TODO block left by the requirement build. **Default ListDetailPanel UI** under Methodology sidebar at position #5 (between Requirements and CRM Candidates per spec ¬ß3.6.1's proposed ordering; a future ordering pass may reorganise the whole group). **Five-column master pane shipped by default** per AC-12 (Identifier / Name / Status / Last Run / Updated). **Last Run column ships with a color cue** per ¬ß3.6.2 UI deviation ‚Äî passing green, failing red, not_run gray, skipped amber (label text always shown; color additive). Implementation: a small `_LastRunColorDelegate` (QStyledItemDelegate subclass) installed via `setItemDelegateForColumn(3, ...)` ‚Äî leaves the shared `MasterPaneDelegate` driving the rest of the row; failing rows render in DemiBold weight for additional 'needs attention' emphasis. **Three-section detail pane** per ¬ß3.6.3 with explicit subsection headers: identity-and-methodology block (identifier read-only, name, description, status combo with hint caption), Test body block (setup, steps, expected ‚Äî placeholders explain each role), Last run block (outcome combo + matching color swatch, last_run_at read-only line, last_run_notes read-only multiline), collapsible Internal notes section (collapsed by default), ReferencesSection. **Record Run action-strip button** opens a transient `TestSpecRecordRunDialog` (modal QDialog subclass ‚Äî not an EntityCrudDialog because the field shape is too narrow); per project_qt_worker_widget_gc_hazard.md the panel calls `deleteLater()` on the dialog after exec returns. CRUD dialogs follow the v0.3 governance-entity pattern: create-dialog excludes identifier (server-assigned); edit-dialog includes identifier as read-only; delete-dialog uses edge-text confirmation (user types TST-NNN) per ¬ß3.6.6. `FieldSchema` does not currently support `widget=datetime` ‚Äî the build prompt's ¬ß12a fallback shipped: `widget=line` with an ISO-8601 placeholder for `test_spec_last_run_at`. 16 testable acceptance criteria; 15 covered by 49 new tests, 1 covered only by live API smoke or follow-on slice (criterion 16 sample CBM-redo records).
decisions.rationale: Convenience record-run endpoint resolves ¬ß3.8.1's open question with the better-shape-for-automation arm: the cost is one more endpoint to maintain; the value is clearer intent for automation callers and a cleaner methodology-vs-execution split. The PATCH endpoint still does the same job (record-run is sugar, not new capability). Three-edge outbound posture is uniform with the rest of the methodology-entity build set per the references-discipline established by DEC-006 / DEC-053. Five-column master with color-cued Last Run is the spec's UI deviation justified by verification-health-at-a-glance per ¬ß3.6.2 ‚Äî the operational value of scanning verification health at the master pane is materially different from scanning methodology lifecycle, drives immediate action (fix the failing tests), and is the primary information consultants want at a glance from the Test Specs panel. Three-section detail pane preserves the setup/action/expected structural hint at the read surface. Record-run modal sub-dialog rather than dedicated full-page edit because the field shape is narrow (outcome + at + notes); the workflow is fast-path 'just record what happened'. Sidebar mid-Methodology position adheres to spec ¬ß3.6.1's proposed ordering; the build prompt explicitly defers final ordering to the build-planning conversation, which has not happened.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Defer record-run convenience endpoint to v0.6+ (rejected; automation pressure is anticipated as the next surface after the v0.5+ build wave settles; shipping it at first release matches the no-defer-cheap-conveniences principle). PATCH-only outcome update (rejected; record-run's clearer intent is worth the one extra endpoint). Single-color outcome column without color cue (rejected per spec ¬ß3.6.2 deviation ‚Äî verification-health-at-a-glance is the primary master-pane value). Four-column master without Last Run (rejected; outcome is the second-most-important master scan after name). Always-expanded Internal notes section (rejected; matches the cross-spec consultant-scratchpad treatment from domain/entity ‚Äî collapsed by default). Inline last_run_at datetime widget in the dialog (rejected; FieldSchema doesn't support widget=datetime, build-prompt ¬ß12a's `line` fallback is correct for v0.5+).
decisions.consequences: Future PI may decide to add `?last_run_outcome=failing` server-side filter once CBM-redo signals scale per ¬ß3.8.2. Future PI may decide to add a `test_run` history entity per ¬ß3.8.3 ‚Äî the schema is consistent with such an entity becoming another writer of `test_spec_last_run_outcome`. Future PI may decide to extend FieldSchema with `widget=datetime` and `visible_when` / `required_when` once a second entity demands them. The cohort ordering convention now reads: Domains, Entities, Processes, Requirements, **Test Specs**, CRM Candidates, Personas, Fields, Manual Configs under the Methodology group. PI-004 RESOLVES on this apply ‚Äî the v0.5+ methodology entity tranche for field / requirement / manual_config / test_spec is complete.
